'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Yuge no Koreo]][[en:draft:Yuge no Koreo]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Yuge no Koreo|Yuge no Koreo]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | Image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848年}})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown
== Notes == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="skf">Manen Suzuki, "Various Genealogies," Vol. 13, Inao Yuge Sukune.</ref> }}
== References == * {{Cite book|editor=Takeda Yukichi, Sato Kenzo|title=Readable Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (上下巻)|publisher=Eijūsha Publishing|date=2009}} * {{Cite book|author=Hōga Toshio|title=Compilation of Ancient Family Lineages|publisher=Research Society on Ancient Clans|date=1986}}

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}.</ref>

By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | Image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848年}})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown
== Notes == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="skf">Manen Suzuki, "Various Genealogies," Vol. 13, Inao Yuge Sukune.</ref> }}

Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Yuge no Koreo]][[en:draft:Yuge no Koreo]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Yuge no Koreo|Yuge no Koreo]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | Image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848年}})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown
== Notes == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="skf">Manen Suzuki, "Various Genealogies," Vol. 13, Inao Yuge Sukune.</ref> }}
== References == * {{Cite book|editor=Takeda Yukichi, Sato Kenzo|title=Readable Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (上下巻)|publisher=Eijūsha Publishing|date=2009}} * {{Cite book|author=Hōga Toshio|title=Compilation of Ancient Family Lineages|publisher=Research Society on Ancient Clans|date=1986}}

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Yuge no Koreo]][[en:draft:Yuge no Koreo]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Yuge no Koreo|Yuge no Koreo]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | Image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848年}})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown
== Notes == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="skf">Manen Suzuki, "Various Genealogies," Vol. 13, Inao Yuge Sukune.</ref> }}
== References == * {{Cite book|editor=Takeda Yukichi, Sato Kenzo|title=Readable Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (上下巻)|publisher=Eijūsha Publishing|date=2009}} * {{Cite book|author=Hōga Toshio|title=Compilation of Ancient Family Lineages|publisher=Research Society on Ancient Clans|date=1986}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Yuge no Koreo]][[en:draft:Yuge no Koreo]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Yuge no Koreo|Yuge no Koreo]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | Image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848年}})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown
== Notes == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="skf">Manen Suzuki, "Various Genealogies," Vol. 13, Inao Yuge Sukune.</ref> }}
== References == * {{Cite book|editor=Takeda Yukichi, Sato Kenzo|title=Readable Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (上下巻)|publisher=Eijūsha Publishing|date=2009}} * {{Cite book|author=Hōga Toshio|title=Compilation of Ancient Family Lineages|publisher=Research Society on Ancient Clans|date=1986}}

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | Image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848年}})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | Image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848年}})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Yuge no Koreo]][[en:draft:Yuge no Koreo]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Yuge no Koreo|Yuge no Koreo]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | Image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848年}})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | Image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848年}})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | Image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848年}})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown
== Notes == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="skf">Manen Suzuki, "Various Genealogies," Vol. 13, Inao Yuge Sukune.</ref> }}
== References == * {{Cite book|editor=Takeda Yukichi, Sato Kenzo|title=Readable Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (上下巻)|publisher=Eijūsha Publishing|date=2009}} * {{Cite book|author=Hōga Toshio|title=Compilation of Ancient Family Lineages|publisher=Research Society on Ancient Clans|date=1986}}

By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|清和天皇}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864年}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873年}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877年}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885年}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908年}}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
