Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848th year }})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864th year }}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873rd year }}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877th year }}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885th year }}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848th year }})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864th year }}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873rd year }}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877th year }}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885th year }}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848th year }})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864th year }}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873rd year }}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877th year }}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885th year }}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873rd year }}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877th year }}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885th year }}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864th year }}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873rd year }}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877th year }}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885th year }}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848th year }})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864th year }}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873rd year }}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877th year }}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885th year }}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Yuge no Koreo]][[en:draft:Yuge no Koreo]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Yuge no Koreo|Yuge no Koreo]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848th year }})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864th year }}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873rd year }}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877th year }}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885th year }}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown
== Notes == {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="skf">Manen Suzuki, "Various Genealogies," Vol. 13, Inao Yuge Sukune.</ref> }}
== References == * {{Cite book|editor=Takeda Yukichi, Sato Kenzo|title=Readable Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku (上下巻)|publisher=Eijūsha Publishing|date=2009}} * {{Cite book|author=Hōga Toshio|title=Compilation of Ancient Family Lineages|publisher=Research Society on Ancient Clans|date=1986}}

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848th year }})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864th year }}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873rd year }}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877th year }}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885th year }}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848th year }})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864th year }}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873rd year }}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877th year }}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885th year }}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848th year }})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864th year }}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873rd year }}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877th year }}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885th year }}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄) | image = | Caption = | Period = {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}} | Birth Date = {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥}} 1 ({{ill|848|ja|848th year }})? | Death Date = {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8, {{ill|May 6 (lunar calendar)|ja|5月6日}} ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}, {{ill|June 7|ja|6月7日}})? | Rank = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭}} | Clan = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏}} | Parent = Father: Yuge Yasuto }}
'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} and {{ill|onmyōji|ja|陰陽師}} of the {{ill|Early Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}.
== Career == Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 6 ({{ill|864|ja|864th year }}), he and his father moved their {{ill|honke|ja|本貫}} from {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}.</ref>
During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 15 ({{ill|873|ja|873rd year }}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊}}. In {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 19 ({{ill|877|ja|877th year }}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|ja|陽成天皇}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Ninna|ja|仁和}} 1 ({{ill|885|ja|885th year }}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi|ja|延喜}} 8 ({{ill|908|ja|908th year }}) at the age of 61.
== Folklore == Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatari|ja|今昔物語集}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>
In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.
== Official Positions == All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} unless noted otherwise: * Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade}}, Onmyōji * Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province * Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Assistant * Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward * Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}, Onmyō Subordinate * Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune * Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province * Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner) * Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō * Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref> * Mother: Unknown

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 弓削是雄 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[平安時代]]前期 | 生誕 = [[嘉祥]]元年（[[848年]]）? | 死没 = [[延喜]]8年[[5月6日 (旧暦)|5月6日]]（[[908年]][[6月7日]]）? | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[従五位|従五位下]]・[[陰陽寮|陰陽頭]] | 主君 = [[清和天皇]]→[[陽成天皇]]→[[光孝天皇]] | 氏族 = [[弓削氏]] | 父母 = 父：弓削安人 | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }} '''弓削 是雄'''（ゆげ の これお）は、[[平安時代]]前期の[[貴族]]・[[陰陽師]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]のち[[宿禰]]。[[正六位|正六位上]]・弓削安人の子。[[官位]]は[[従五位|従五位下]]・[[陰陽寮|陰陽頭]]。
== 経歴 == [[清和天皇|清和朝]]前半に[[陰陽寮]]の[[陰陽師]]を務め、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]6年（[[864年]]）父・安人らと共に[[本貫]]を[[播磨国]][[飾磨郡]]から[[河内国]][[大県郡]]に改める<ref>この時の位階は[[従八位|従八位下]]。</ref>。清和朝後半は陰陽允・陰陽権助と引き続き陰陽寮の[[官人]]を務め、貞観15年（[[873年]]）には再び本貫を移し右京三条二坊に貫附されている。
貞観19年（[[877年]]）正月に[[陽成天皇]]の即位に伴って[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]に昇叙され、同年12月には連姓から宿禰姓に[[改姓]]する。また同年4月1日に夜間の[[日蝕]]が発生したが、事前に[[中務省]]から[[上奏|奏上]]があり[[廃務]]が行われた。これに関して、事前に[[官司]]が夜間の日蝕を奏上する事への是非について[[暦博士]]に下問があった。これに対して、陰陽頭兼暦博士・[[家原郷好]]と共に、夜間の日蝕が発生する際には予め中務省に報告がなされる例となっている旨の言上をしている<ref>『日本三代実録』元慶元年4月1日条</ref>。
[[元慶]]6年（[[882年]]）内位の従五位下に叙せられ、[[仁和]]元年（[[885年]]）[[陰陽頭]]に昇格した。一説では[[延喜]]8年（[[908年]]）5月6日卒去。享年61。
== 説話 == [[占い]]の技術に定評があったらしく、あるとき同宿者に対する[[夢占い]]を行って危機を知らせてその命を救った、との[[説話]]が『[[今昔物語集]]』にある<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話「天文博士弓削是男占夢語」</ref>。 :今は昔、穀蔵院の使として[[封戸]]を催促するために東国に行っていた伴世継は、帰京の途中で[[近江国]]の[[勢多駅]]に宿泊したが、ちょうど同国[[国司]]から[[属星|大属星]]を祭るために呼ばれていた、[[陰陽師]][[天文博士]]'''弓削是雄'''と同宿した。
:世継はその夜に悪夢をみた。目が覚めてから是雄に夢の吉凶を占ってもらったところ、是雄は「明日家へ帰ってはいけない。あなたを殺そうとする者が家にいる」と言う。しかし、世継は「長く東国に行っていたので早く家に帰りたいし、多くの公物・私物を持っていてさらに[[家来|従者]]も引き連れているので、ここに留まることなどできない、どうやったら難を避けることができるか」と尋ねる。是雄は「どうしても帰りたいと思うのなら、あなたを殺害しようとする者は家の[[鬼門|丑寅]]の角の場所に隠れている。そこで、あなたが家に到着したら従者をそのまま残しておいて、あなた一人で矢を持って丑寅の角の隠れ場所になりそうな所に向かって弓を引いて、『私が東国より帰るのを待って、今日私を殺害しようとしていることはわかっている。早く出てこい。出でこなければ速やかに射殺してやるぞ』と言いなさい。そうすれば、法術で隠れていようとも、自然と顕れ出てくるでしょう」と教えた。
:世継は是男の助言を得て、翌日京に急ぎ戻った。家に到着すると家の者は「お帰りなさいませ」と非常にやかましい大声をあげた。世継は従者を家に入れないまま待機させておいて、矢を持って丑寅の角を見回すと一間ほどの場所に菰が掛けてある。「ここだな」と思って、弓を引いて差し当てて「私が京に戻るのを待って、今日私を殺害しようとしていることはわかっている。早く出てこい。出でこなければ速やかに射殺してやるぞ」と言った。
:そうすると、菰の中から一人の法師が出てきた。すぐに世継は従者を呼んで法師を捕らえさせて強く問うと、法師は「隠しておくべき事でもない、私の主人の御房は年来この殿の上（世継の妻）と通じていた。今日、世継が帰京することを聞いて『帰宅をしたところを必ず殺して』と殿の上が仰ったので隠れていたのだが、事前に知っていたとは」と言った。
:世継はこれを聞いて、前世の果報のおかげで高名な是雄と同宿して命が救われたことを喜んだ。また、是雄の占いが的中したことを感じて、まず是雄の方向に向かって拝んだ。その後、法師を[[検非違使]]に引き渡し、妻とは離縁した。
:これを思うに、年来の妻と言っても油断してはいけない、女性の心はこういったこともある。また、是雄の占いは不思議である。昔はこのようなものすごい陰陽師がいたものである、と語り伝えたことだ。
== 官歴 == 注記のないものは『[[日本三代実録]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[従八位|従八位下]]。[[陰陽師]] *[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]6年（[[864年]]） 8月8日：[[播磨国]][[飾磨郡]]から[[河内国]][[大県郡]]に[[本貫|移貫]] *時期不詳：[[正七位|正七位上]]。[[陰陽寮|陰陽允]] *貞観15年（[[873年]]） 12月1日：右京三条二坊に移貫 *時期不詳：[[正六位|正六位上]]。陰陽権助兼[[播磨国#国司|播磨権少掾]] *貞観19年（[[877年]]） 正月3日：[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]。12月25日：[[連]]姓から[[宿禰]]姓に[[改姓]] *時期不詳：兼[[備前国#国司|備前権掾]] *[[元慶]]6年（[[882年]]） 正月7日：従五位下（[[内位]]） *[[仁和]]元年（[[885年]]） 4月27日：[[陰陽頭]] *[[延喜]]8年（[[908年]]） 5月6日：[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]<ref name="skf" />
== 系譜 == *父：弓削安人<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観6年8月8日条</ref> *母：不詳
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="skf">鈴木真年『諸系譜』第13冊,稲生 弓削宿禰</ref> }}
== 出典 == *[[武田祐吉]]、[[佐藤謙三]]訳『読み下し 日本三代実録』（上下巻）、[[戎光祥出版]]、2009年 *[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年

:世継はその夜に悪夢をみた。目が覚めてから是雄に夢の吉凶を占ってもらったところ、是雄は「明日家へ帰ってはいけない。あなたを殺そうとする者が家にいる」と言う。しかし、世継は「長く東国に行っていたので早く家に帰りたいし、多くの公物・私物を持っていてさらに[[家来|従者]]も引き連れているので、ここに留まることなどできない、どうやったら難を避けることができるか」と尋ねる。是雄は「どうしても帰りたいと思うのなら、あなたを殺害しようとする者は家の[[鬼門|丑寅]]の角の場所に隠れている。そこで、あなたが家に到着したら従者をそのまま残しておいて、あなた一人で矢を持って丑寅の角の隠れ場所になりそうな所に向かって弓を引いて、『私が東国より帰るのを待って、今日私を殺害しようとしていることはわかっている。早く出てこい。出でこなければ速やかに射殺してやるぞ』と言いなさい。そうすれば、法術で隠れていようとも、自然と顕れ出てくるでしょう」と教えた。
:世継は是男の助言を得て、翌日京に急ぎ戻った。家に到着すると家の者は「お帰りなさいませ」と非常にやかましい大声をあげた。世継は従者を家に入れないまま待機させておいて、矢を持って丑寅の角を見回すと一間ほどの場所に菰が掛けてある。「ここだな」と思って、弓を引いて差し当てて「私が京に戻るのを待って、今日私を殺害しようとしていることはわかっている。早く出てこい。出でこなければ速やかに射殺してやるぞ」と言った。
:そうすると、菰の中から一人の法師が出てきた。すぐに世継は従者を呼んで法師を捕らえさせて強く問うと、法師は「隠しておくべき事でもない、私の主人の御房は年来この殿の上（世継の妻）と通じていた。今日、世継が帰京することを聞いて『帰宅をしたところを必ず殺して』と殿の上が仰ったので隠れていたのだが、事前に知っていたとは」と言った。
:世継はこれを聞いて、前世の果報のおかげで高名な是雄と同宿して命が救われたことを喜んだ。また、是雄の占いが的中したことを感じて、まず是雄の方向に向かって拝んだ。その後、法師を[[検非違使]]に引き渡し、妻とは離縁した。
:これを思うに、年来の妻と言っても油断してはいけない、女性の心はこういったこともある。また、是雄の占いは不思議である。昔はこのようなものすごい陰陽師がいたものである、と語り伝えたことだ。
== 官歴 == 注記のないものは『[[日本三代実録]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[従八位|従八位下]]。[[陰陽師]] *[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]6年（[[864年]]） 8月8日：[[播磨国]][[飾磨郡]]から[[河内国]][[大県郡]]に[[本貫|移貫]] *時期不詳：[[正七位|正七位上]]。[[陰陽寮|陰陽允]] *貞観15年（[[873年]]） 12月1日：右京三条二坊に移貫 *時期不詳：[[正六位|正六位上]]。陰陽権助兼[[播磨国#国司|播磨権少掾]] *貞観19年（[[877年]]） 正月3日：[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]。12月25日：[[連]]姓から[[宿禰]]姓に[[改姓]] *時期不詳：兼[[備前国#国司|備前権掾]] *[[元慶]]6年（[[882年]]） 正月7日：従五位下（[[内位]]） *[[仁和]]元年（[[885年]]） 4月27日：[[陰陽頭]] *[[延喜]]8年（[[908年]]） 5月6日：[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]<ref name="skf" />
== 系譜 == *父：弓削安人<ref>『日本三代実録』貞観6年8月8日条</ref> *母：不詳
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|refs= <ref name="skf">鈴木真年『諸系譜』第13冊,稲生 弓削宿禰</ref> }}
== 出典 == *[[武田祐吉]]、[[佐藤謙三]]訳『読み下し 日本三代実録』（上下巻）、[[戎光祥出版]]、2009年 *[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年

{{Infobox noble
| Name          = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄)
| image         = 
| Caption       = 
| Period        = [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]
| Birth Date    = [[:en:Kashō|Kashō]] 1 ([[:en:848|848]])?
| Death Date    = [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8, [[:en:May 6|May 6 (lunar calendar)]] ([[:en:908|908]], [[:en:June 7|June 7]])?
| Rank          = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|qq=|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}
| Clan          = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}
| Parent        = Father: Yuge Yasuto
}}

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] unless noted otherwise:
* Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, Onmyōji
* Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, Onmyō Assistant
* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate
* Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune
* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
* Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō
* Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

== Genealogy ==
* Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref>
* Mother: Unknown

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] unless noted otherwise:

* Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, Onmyō Assistant
* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate
* Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune
* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
* Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō
* Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

{{Infobox noble
| Name          = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄)
| image         = 
| Caption       =

| Rank          = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] unless noted otherwise:
* Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, Onmyōji
* Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, Onmyō Assistant
* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate
* Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune
* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
* Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō
* Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] unless noted otherwise:
* Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, Onmyōji
* Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, Onmyō Assistant
* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate
* Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune
* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
* Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō
* Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

== Genealogy ==

* Mother: Unknown

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

{{Infobox noble
| Name          = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄)
| image         = 
| Caption       = 
| Period        = [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]
| Birth Date    = [[:en:Kashō|Kashō]] 1 ([[:en:848|848]])?
| Death Date    = [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8, [[:en:May 6|May 6 (lunar calendar)]] ([[:en:908|908]], [[:en:June 7|June 7]])?
| Rank          = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}
| Clan          = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}
| Parent        = Father: Yuge Yasuto
}}

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] unless noted otherwise:
* Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, Onmyōji
* Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, Onmyō Assistant
* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate
* Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune
* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
* Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō
* Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

== Genealogy ==
* Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref>
* Mother: Unknown
