'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] unless noted otherwise:
* Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, Onmyōji
* Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, Onmyō Assistant
* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate
* Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune
* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
* Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō
* Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

== Genealogy ==

* Mother: Unknown

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

{{Infobox noble
| Name          = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄)
| image         = 
| Caption       = 
| Period        = [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]
| Birth Date    = [[:en:Kashō|Kashō]] 1 ([[:en:848|848]])?
| Death Date    = [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8, [[:en:May 6|May 6 (lunar calendar)]] ([[:en:908|908]], [[:en:June 7|June 7]])?
| Rank          = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}
| Clan          = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}
| Parent        = Father: Yuge Yasuto
}}

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] unless noted otherwise:
* Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, Onmyōji
* Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, Onmyō Assistant
* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate
* Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune
* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
* Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō
* Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

== Genealogy ==

* Mother: Unknown

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] unless noted otherwise:
* Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, Onmyōji
* Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, Onmyō Assistant
* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate
* Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune
* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
* Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō
* Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

== Genealogy ==
* Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref>
* Mother: Unknown

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. == Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

{{Infobox noble
| Name          = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄)
| image         = 
| Caption       = 
| Period        = [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]
| Birth Date    = [[:en:Kashō|Kashō]] 1 ([[:en:848|848]])?
| Death Date    = [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8, [[:en:May 6|May 6 (lunar calendar)]] ([[:en:908|908]], [[:en:June 7|June 7]])?
| Rank          = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}
| Clan          = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}
| Parent        = Father: Yuge Yasuto
}}

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] unless noted otherwise:
* Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, Onmyōji
* Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, Onmyō Assistant
* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate
* Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune
* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
* Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō
* Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

== Genealogy ==
* Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref>
* Mother: Unknown

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] unless noted otherwise:
* Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, Onmyōji
* Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, Onmyō Assistant
* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate
* Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune
* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
* Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō
* Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

{{Infobox noble
| Name          = Kureo Yuge (弓削 是雄)
| image         = 
| Caption       = 
| Period        = [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]
| Birth Date    = [[:en:Kashō|Kashō]] 1 ([[:en:848|848]])?
| Death Date    = [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8, [[:en:May 6|May 6 (lunar calendar)]] ([[:en:908|908]], [[:en:June 7|June 7]])?
| Rank          = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}
| Clan          = {{ill|Yuge clan|ja|弓削氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge clan|qq=}}
| Parent        = Father: Yuge Yasuto
}}

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] unless noted otherwise:
* Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, Onmyōji
* Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, Onmyō Assistant
* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate
* Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune
* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
* Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō
* Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

== Genealogy ==
* Father: {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 6 (864), August 8 entry.</ref>
* Mother: Unknown

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] unless noted otherwise:
* Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, Onmyōji
* Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, Onmyō Assistant
* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate
* Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune
* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
* Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō
* Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

| Rank          = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}

}}

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] unless noted otherwise:
* Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, Onmyōji
* Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, Onmyō Assistant
* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate
* Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune
* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
* Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō
* Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

== Genealogy ==

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] unless noted otherwise:
* Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, Onmyōji
* Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, Onmyō Assistant
* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate
* Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune
* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
* Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō
* Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

| Rank          = {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, {{ill|Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|ja|陰陽頭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658275}}

}}

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] unless noted otherwise:
* Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, Onmyōji
* Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, Onmyō Assistant
* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate
* Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune
* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
* Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō
* Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

== Genealogy ==

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] and [[:en:Onmyōji|onmyōji]] of the [[:en:Heian period|Early Heian period]]. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 6 ([[:en:864|864]]), he and his father moved their [[:en:Korean clans|honke]] from [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] to [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]].<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 15 ([[:en:873|873]]), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 19 ([[:en:877|877]]), upon [[:en:Emperor Yōzei|Emperor Yōzei]]'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In [[:en:Ninna|Ninna]] 1 ([[:en:885|885]]), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in [[:en:Engi (era)|Engi]] 8 ([[:en:908|908]]) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the [[:en:Konjaku Monogatarishū|Konjaku Monogatari]] recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] unless noted otherwise:
* Undated: {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}, Onmyōji
* Jōgan 6 (864): Honke moved from Harima Province to Kawachi Province
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, Onmyō Assistant
* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate
* Jōgan 19 (877): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} (Outer). Clan name changed to Sukune
* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
* Ninna 1 (885): Appointed Chief of the Bureau of Onmyō
* Engi 8 (908): Passed away<ref name="skf" />

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=onmyōji}} of the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Early Heian period}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|864|en|864|WD=Q25041|lt=864}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|Korean clans|en|Korean clans|WD=Q846706|lt=honke}} from {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} 15 ({{ill|873|en|873|WD=Q30369|lt=873}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} 19 ({{ill|877|en|877|WD=Q23905|lt=877}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|en|Emperor Yōzei|WD=Q349280|lt=Emperor Yōzei}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninna}} 1 ({{ill|885|en|885|WD=Q24894|lt=885}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi (era)|en|Engi (era)|WD=Q1374325|lt=Engi}} 8 ({{ill|908|en|908|WD=Q30463|lt=908}}) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|Konjaku Monogatarishū|WD=Q1507085|lt=Konjaku Monogatari}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

== Official Positions ==
All details are from the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} unless noted otherwise:

'''Kureo Yuge''' (弓削 是雄) was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble}} and {{ill|Onmyōji|en|Onmyōji|WD=Q2274163|lt=onmyōji}} of the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Early Heian period}}. He held the title {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} and served as Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. He was the son of {{ill|Yuge Yasuto|ja|弓削安人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge Yasuto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career ==
Kureo began his service in the Bureau of Onmyō (陰陽寮) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|Emperor Seiwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, working as an onmyōji. In {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} 6 ({{ill|864|en|864|WD=Q25041|lt=864}}), he and his father moved their {{ill|Korean clans|en|Korean clans|WD=Q846706|lt=honke}} from {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}} to {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province}}.<ref>At the time, Kureo held the Rank of {{ill|Junior Eighth Rank|ja|従八位下|1=Junior Eighth Rank|lt=Junior Eighth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従八位}}.</ref>

During Emperor Seiwa’s later reign, he continued in various roles within the Bureau of Onmyō. By {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} 15 ({{ill|873|en|873|WD=Q30369|lt=873}}), he relocated his honke again to {{ill|Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja|右京三条二坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} 19 ({{ill|877|en|877|WD=Q23905|lt=877}}), upon {{ill|Emperor Yōzei|en|Emperor Yōzei|WD=Q349280|lt=Emperor Yōzei}}'s accession, he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, and his clan's name was changed from "Ren" (連) to "Sukune" (宿禰).<ref>『日本三代実録』Jōgan 19 (877), April 1 entry.</ref>

In {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninna}} 1 ({{ill|885|en|885|WD=Q24894|lt=885}}), he became Chief of the {{ill|Yin-Yang Bureau|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|en:draft|Yin-Yang Bureau|lt=Yin-Yang Bureau|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}}. A record suggests he passed away in {{ill|Engi (era)|en|Engi (era)|WD=Q1374325|lt=Engi}} 8 ({{ill|908|en|908|WD=Q30463|lt=908}}) at the age of 61.

== Folklore ==
Kureo was reputed for his divination skills. A story in the {{ill|Konjaku Monogatarishū|en|Konjaku Monogatarishū|WD=Q1507085|lt=Konjaku Monogatari}} recounts his dream interpretation that saved a man's life.<ref>『今昔物語集』巻24第14話 "天文博士弓削是男占夢語".</ref>

In this tale, Kureo warns his fellow traveler, Ban no Tsugutsugu, against returning home due to a prophetic dream. Kureo instructs Tsugutsugu on how to confront the danger. Following the advice, Tsugutsugu discovers an assassin hidden in his house and averts his fate, confirming Kureo’s prophetic prowess.

* Jōgan 15 (873): Honke moved to Ukyo Sanjō Second Ward
* Undated: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}, Onmyō Subordinate

* Undated: Assistant in Bizen Province
* Genkei 6 (882): Promoted to Junior Fifth Rank (Inner)
